name: Zahir ibn Amr
title: Kindi Warrior
shortDescription: A brave and veteran man of Kufa who joined the Imam in Mecca and fought manfully on Ashura until martyrdom.
fullStory:
Zahir ibn Amr al-Kindi was a brave, veteran man and a known warrior of Kufa. He was a loyal companion of 'Amr ibn Hamiq al-Khuza'i' (a noble companion of the Prophet) and had a brilliant record of defending the righteousness of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS).

After Amr ibn Hamiq was martyred by Muawiyah, Zahir was pursued by the government but bravely continued his life in hiding until 60 AH, when he joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan during the Hajj in Mecca.

As a pious old man and an experienced warrior, he headed to Karbala with immense love for the Ahl al-Bayt. On Ashura, with the start of Yazid's army's all-out attack and arrow barrage, Zahir ibn Amr drew his sword.

He stood on the front line of defense for the Imam's tents and, despite his advanced age, fought like a powerful youth until he attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى زاهِرٍ مَوْلى عَمْرِو بْنِ الحَمِقِ الكِنْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Zahir, companion of Amr ibn Hamiq al-Kindi.
